expectations.text: Response does not contain substring `DB really is that full`.
expectations.evidence: Substring 'DB really is that full' absent in response.md

expectations.text: Response does not contain substring `your DB has 2735`.
expectations.evidence: Substring 'your DB has 2735' absent in response.md

expectations.text: Identifies that the 2735 is the full table — not the sessions, not a bug in the count
expectations.evidence: Says '2735 is almost certainly the entire DecodingOutput master table, not the 4 sessions you asked for. The restriction silently did nothing.'

expectations.text: Explains the silent no-op: nwb_file_name is not on the merge master's heading, DataJoint drops it
expectations.evidence: States 'nwb_file_name is not in that heading, so DataJoint applies a no-op restriction ... and & returns the full master table. No error, no warning'.

expectations.text: Shows `merge_restrict` per-file as the correct shape (NOT `merge_get_part`, since the question is about counting not loading)
expectations.evidence: Provides `total = sum(len(DecodingOutput.merge_restrict({'nwb_file_name': f})) for f in files)` and an explicit section 'don't reach for merge_get_part here'.

expectations.text: Mentions the five merge masters by name OR at least that this applies to any table with `merge_id` as its PK
expectations.evidence: Lists all five merge masters (SpikeSortingOutput, LFPOutput, PositionOutput, LinearizedPositionOutput, DecodingOutput).

expectations.text: Does not propose `merge_get_part` for a counting task (that would raise on multi-source)
expectations.evidence: Has dedicated section 'Important: don't reach for merge_get_part here' and explains it 'will either raise (multiple sources) or only count one source'.
